intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through using the PDE XML Cleanup to optimize Eclipse plugin descriptor files (plugin.xml, feature.xml) by normalizing whitespace and converting empty elements to self-closing tags.

item.title: Understand PDE XML Cleanup
item.description:
Eclipse PDE XML files often accumulate unnecessary whitespace, redundant attributes, and verbose empty elements over time. The PDE XML Cleanup:
• Removes trailing whitespace from element content
• Collapses empty elements to self-closing tags (e.g. <element></element> → <element/>)
• Normalizes indentation for consistent formatting
• Preserves all semantic content
This keeps plugin descriptors clean and reduces unnecessary diff noise in version control.

item.title: Run the Cleanup via the Context Menu
item.description:
The easiest way to run the PDE XML cleanup is via the context menu:
1. In the Package Explorer or Project Explorer, right-click on a plugin.xml or feature.xml file
2. Choose Clean Up PDE XML from the context menu
The file is reformatted and saved immediately. You can also select multiple PDE XML files and run the cleanup on all of them at once.

item.title: Run the Cleanup via the Main Menu (Optional)
item.description:
The cleanup can also be triggered from the main menu:
1. Open a plugin.xml or feature.xml file in the XML editor
2. Choose Source → Clean Up PDE XML from the main menu bar
This is equivalent to using the context menu and applies the same formatting rules.

item.title: Review the Optimized XML
item.description:
After running the cleanup, verify the XML changes:
• Empty extension elements use self-closing syntax
• Indentation is consistent (no mixed tabs/spaces)
• No trailing whitespace in element text content
• XML semantics and all extension registrations are fully preserved
Open a diff in the Git Staging view (Window → Show View → Other… → Git → Git Staging) to review the formatting-only changes before committing.
